skill.name: meeting-minutes-generator
skill.description: Generates structured meeting minutes from text transcripts. Use when the user provides text content and wants a structured summary with a signature.
skill.summary: Converts meeting transcripts into structured minutes with participants, key topics, decisions, action items, and a timestamped signature.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 37 static analysis findings are false positives. The 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' alerts (15 instances) are false matches against markdown formatting and template variable syntax. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' alerts (20 instances) are markdown code fences and inline formatting, not shell commands. The 'Hardcoded URL' is a legitimate source repository link. This is a pure instruction-only skill with no executable scripts, network calls, or external commands. No prompt injection or malicious patterns were found.

content.user_title: Generate structured meeting minutes from transcripts
content.value_statement: Manually converting meeting transcripts into organized minutes is time-consuming and inconsistent. This skill automatically extracts participants, key topics, decisions, action items, and next steps into a timestamped structured format.
actual_capabilities: Extracts participant names from meeting transcripts
Identifies key discussion topics and decisions made
Generates action items with responsible person and deadline
Produces a timestamped structured minutes document
Handles multiple meeting types: Work Report, Client Communication, and Other
limitations: Cannot process audio or video files directly; requires text transcripts
May mark participants as 'Not specified' if names are not mentioned in the transcript
Does not handle real-time transcription; works with completed transcripts only
Limited to content explicitly stated in the transcript - cannot infer unspoken context

use_cases.title: Team standup recap
use_cases.description: A team lead pastes a daily standup transcript and receives organized minutes with decisions and action items for each member.

use_cases.title: Client meeting documentation
use_cases.description: A consultant pastes a client call transcript and generates professional meeting minutes with clear next steps and commitments.

use_cases.title: Research group discussion notes
use_cases.description: A researcher submits a lab meeting transcript and gets structured minutes with key scientific decisions and assigned follow-ups.

prompt_templates.title: Basic minutes from transcript
prompt_templates.prompt:
Generate structured meeting minutes from the following transcript. Include participants, key topics, decisions, action items, and next steps. End with a timestamp.

Transcript: [paste transcript here]
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick minutes generation with default settings

prompt_templates.title: Minutes with meeting title and type
prompt_templates.prompt:
Generate structured meeting minutes from this transcript. The meeting title is [title]. The meeting type is [Work Report / Client Communication / Other]. Follow the standard format.

Transcript: [paste transcript here]
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding metadata context for better classification

prompt_templates.title: Minutes with custom output file
prompt_templates.prompt:
Generate structured meeting minutes from the following transcript. Meeting title: [title]. Type: [meeting type]. Save the output to a file named [custom_filename].md with the timestamp included.

Transcript: [paste transcript here]
prompt_templates.scenario: Saving results to a file for sharing or record keeping

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive review with validation
prompt_templates.prompt:
Generate structured meeting minutes from this transcript. Include all standard sections. After generation, provide a validation summary that lists what was checked, what assumptions were made, and any limitations of the output.

Meeting title: [title]
Type: [meeting type]

Transcript: [paste transcript here]
prompt_templates.scenario: Production use where output quality verification is required

output_examples.input:
Sarah: Let's discuss the Q2 marketing budget. John, what's the status on the social media campaign?
John: We have launched 3 out of 5 planned campaigns. The remaining two need approval for an additional $5,000.
Sarah: Approved. Let's allocate the funds. Maria, can you handle the vendor setup?
Maria: Yes, I will contact the vendor by Friday and have campaigns running by next Wednesday.
output_examples.output:
Meeting Title: Q2 Marketing Budget Review
Meeting Type: Work Report
Participants: Sarah, John, Maria

Key Topics Discussed:
- Q2 marketing budget allocation
- Social media campaign status (3 of 5 launched)
- Additional funding request for remaining campaigns

Decisions Made:
- Additional $5,000 approved for remaining campaigns

Action Items:
- Maria - Contact vendor for campaign setup - Friday
- Maria - Complete campaign launch - Next Wednesday

Next Steps:
- Allocate additional budget to remaining campaigns

Prepared Time: 2026-05-28 10:30:00

output_examples.input:
Client: We need the dashboard prototype by end of month.
Dev: That timeline is tight. We can deliver a functional MVP but without the analytics module.
Client: That works. Focus on core charts and navigation.
PM: I will update the project plan and share revised milestones by tomorrow.
output_examples.output:
Meeting Title: Not specified
Meeting Type: Client Communication
Participants: Client, Dev, PM

Key Topics Discussed:
- Dashboard prototype delivery timeline
- Scope of MVP vs full feature set
- Analytics module deferral

Decisions Made:
- MVP will exclude analytics module
- Focus on core charts and navigation first

Action Items:
- PM - Update project plan with revised milestones - Tomorrow

Next Steps:
- Deliver functional MVP by end of month

Prepared Time: 2026-05-28 14:15:00

best_practices: Paste the full transcript without edits so the skill can extract all participant names and decisions accurately.
Specify the meeting title and meeting type for better organized output, especially when archiving multiple sets of minutes.
Review the generated action items to confirm each task has a named responsible person and a clear deadline.
anti_patterns: Do not paste partial or heavily redacted transcripts, as this leads to missing participants and incomplete action items.
Do not use this skill for real-time transcription during live meetings; it is designed for completed transcripts only.
Do not ask the skill to fabricate missing details; the output must reflect only what is present in the transcript.

faq.question: What input formats does this skill accept?
faq.answer: This skill accepts plain text transcripts. It does not process audio, video, or PDF files directly. Convert your recording to text first.

faq.question: Can this skill handle multiple meeting types?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports three meeting types: Work Report, Client Communication, and Other. You can specify the type when providing the transcript.

faq.question: What happens if participant names are not mentioned?
faq.answer: If names are unclear from the transcript, the output will show 'Not specified' in the Participants section.

faq.question: What if there are no decisions or action items?
faq.answer: The skill will output 'None identified' for any section where no relevant content is found in the transcript.

faq.question: Does this skill save output to a file automatically?
faq.answer: By default the skill returns the formatted minutes in the chat. You can request it to save to a file by specifying a filename in your prompt.

faq.question: Is the timestamp in the output accurate?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill retrieves the current system time when generating the output and includes it in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format.
